tokens: Okay, Bob, I'd like the range and bearing to the...We're roll...We...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How did you get in reverse?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We're rolling, and I'd like the range and bearing to the next...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We want a heading of around 125, and there's going to be a small turn, I think it's at SWP Crater at 225 and 3.4, there'll be a slight turn.
That's a heading of 125 is what you should start out on.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I thought we were bypassing SWP.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I got my...Man, I tell you, this little navigation map I've got on my cuff checklist (
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Sure hard to tell where north is on it, though.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Trying my best to keep you out of those slopes.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're pretty close now to...No, we're not.
We're still about 100 meters, I think, from where the break in slope is with the plains.
But we're away from the block population except for scattered...(except for) two great big blocks out ahead of us, this side of the SWP Crater.
But the average population is down to the 1 percent or less, again.
(That is, one percent of the surface is covered by rocks ("blocks") bigger than about one centimeter.) That average population really never changed up in here.
Just the big blocks were around.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Look at the size of those things (the big blocks)!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Laughs) And it looks like they're probably the same thing that we sampled.
They have the inclusions in them, white inclusions.
They look like a mixture of the gray of the re-crystallized breccia, and the tan-gray of the anorthositic gabbro.
That's the other side of Cochise there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Cochise is certainly a shallow crater, although we knew that.
It doesn't have any...It only has one place I can see that has any blocks on the inner wall of Cochise.
Otherwise, it has a surface much like what we're driving on - for walls and for the floor.
One place on the south-southeast wall, there is a concentration of blocks much like we saw in Henry or in Horatio.
But the rest of the crater seems to be pretty well mantled.
(Pause) Van Serg is a very blocky rim crater, big blocks up on the rim.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Do you have a lot of static, Jack, or is it just me?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, I think it's just you.
People are always giving you static.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, if you are still reading me, I'm looking at the Sculptured Hills, and I still have that (impression of an) old man, wrinkled-face appearance, even up close at this Sun angle.
And those wrinkles go from, generally, upslope at the west to downslope at the east.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, are you...No, you're right at the edge of Cochise.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Cochise is up at...See that rim where those blocks are?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, I'll bet you that's Cochise up there.
We've got to go quite a ways yet to get to...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How about a range and bearing there, guys?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: This sideslope driving is really a tough...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How about a range and bearing?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And you should be somewhat north of Cochise there, as per planning, although you may be cutting south to try (to avoid) going directly up-Sun.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I think Cochise is over that rim.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Every bump you go over on a sideslope is just accentuated.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, we don't think you're anywhere near Cochise.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I think it's over that rim up there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's just a big, shallow depression.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: All I'm doing is flying the 3.4-kilometer arc right now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There's another one of those deep craters that doesn't have a blocky rim.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's one of the more striking characteristics of the mantle are these craters that look, as far as the diameter-to-depth ratio is concerned, like they ought to be fairly young.
But there's no blocks on the rim, and they seem to have this mantled appearance, just like some of the large craters.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: As I look up Wessex Cleft from just about abeam of it...(Turning to avoid an obstacle) Let me get over here...It still shows me an albedo change and a surface wrinkle-texture change.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You've got it at the same Sun angle more or less, on part of it there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You guys may be getting just a little far north...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It's sort of a perfectly formed saddle in there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...You may want to head just a little south to avoid running right into the middle of SWP Crater.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I think we see it now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's my...That's what I'm shooting for, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm just flying a 3.4 mile...(correcting himself) or, kilometer arc.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I was going to suggest that.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, there's something I haven't mentioned, but if one had time on the next (lunar) program (after Apollo)...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think that's SWP right there, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...you can sample secondary craters, and they tend to have blocks either in them or on one rim, suggesting that you could tell directions if you put your mind to it.
Directions of where the secondary came from.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Did we ever get a piece of glass in place?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's what I was trying to protect in the SRC yesterday.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's coming right up, and I'll go along the southern rim.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I wish I wouldn't lose...(To himself) Start concentrating.
I'm forgetting to take my pictures.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Don't forget to take your pictures.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'll tell you, if we don't concentrate, we end up...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll quit thinking and just take pictures.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's a crater...That double pit-bottom crater.
That's the first one of those I've seen.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Right here, Jack, you're going to be able to peek right over the top of SWP.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: SWP's a bigger hole than I thought it was.
(Pause) SWP even has some blocks in the wall.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, but the eastern and southeastern rim of SWP are just continuous...Ooh!...They're just continuous with the slopes of the Sculptured Hills.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) How does 238/4.2 sound for the beginning of (Station) 8?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: May have to take these slopes just the most comfortable way we can.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: 238 and 4.0 (is what) we're expecting for Station 8, the beginning of it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And remember again, Station 8 is a very flexible area.
You just get to a place where it looks like it's feasible to sample the Sculptured Hills (material).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) Let me tell you, this Rover is a machine.
I don't know if it saw that hill we're climbing, but I did.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm trying to get around SWP over here and start hitting the...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (The) East Massif has outcrops on it (that) I can see now on the north side.
And they, (like the North Massif outcrops), also tend to have linear upper terminations.
And some of those line up as if there's roughly horizontal structure within the upper one-half of the East Massif.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Gene) Go by that little dark crater over there.
(To Bob) There's a very blocky-rimmed small crater, that's a dark-rim crater instead of a bright-rim (crater) like we'd seen some around that looked fresh.
It partly may be the angle at which we're approaching it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, we're on the southeastern rim of SWP at 226 and 3.6.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Gene) Why don't we get some samples of that material in there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Keep driving toward the rim and then just (do) a shallow curve.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Right...Just keep going, and I'll stop you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's a highly fragmental, small crater about 30 or 40 meters across, right on the southeastern rim of SWP.
And most of the fragments are football size and smaller, and they're very angular.
And probably the inside of that rim is...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I guess that's going to be about 70 percent covered on the inside of the rim with these things.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's all instant rock (soil compacted in the impact), but the crater rim looks dark compared to other fresh craters like this that we've seen.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, 17, we're hoping to go to station 8A...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Your wheels are just chewing those things (the clods) up.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...the northernmost section of Station 8, if we can, of course.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I think...I think we ought to head just about...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well...I think we ought to get below the highest peak up there because that seems to have the rocks on it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Of course, I don't know whether that came down.
Doesn't look like it may have come down from the top.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's certainly not like the old North and South Massifs.
(Pause) Yeah, there's one big rock over there.
That doesn't look like...It might be a(n exotic)...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's about where the station is, anyway.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think we're starting to see blocks.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, this probably is...We can get the other smaller population around it.
I'm worried about that one being exotic to the Sculptured Hills.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, it doesn't look like it rolled...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: But I don't see any others, do you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, there's some small ones up in there.
Off to about the 2 o'clock position (relative to the direction of travel).
We're going to have to be satisfied with small ones.
There's some big ones way up on the slope.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause; they've hit the crater) You're (low-gain) antenna (needs to be adjusted)...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, let me get it for them.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think it's worth...There's smaller ones around here, too, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That looks like subfloor from here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: What's it (the immediate area) look like?
If it doesn't look worthwhile stopping, I'll move on up over there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...try to get up to some of those.
I don't know whether we can or not.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: See that...Those two up there would be reasonably well up the slope.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, no parking constraints on the battery?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I have to park about 045 because I've got to be pointing uphill so we can get out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How about just the rim of that little crater there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, this is so level right here, Jack, I'm going to just park it...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I was just thinking on top of that crater is closer to the...That's level, too, on the rim.
And it'll give them a good view of the sampling area.
(Pause) I think we can...If we work on those blocks there, we're in pretty good shape.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, we're directly downhill, and that is (to say, directly downhill) from the highest point that I could see up on this first Sculptured Hill.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, I'm parked at (a heading of) 026; bearing is 226; distance, 6.6; range, 4.0; amp-hours, 85 and 80; battery is 1...I think it's 115; and the motors are all off-scale low.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...0 (and) 230 on the forward; and off-scale low (on the left rear); 220 on the right rear.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We'd like to get the usual dusting here.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I just about rolled downhill again.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Laughs) I am pointing uphill, aren't I?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, at least we don't have a sideslope.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: But we do want the LCRU, and the TV camera, and the TCU (Television Control Unit) dusted.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And, 17, we'd like the SEP blankets opened and dusted one more time.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I think you're a dreamer, Bob, but I'll do it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's going to take me a little while to get this dusted.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, the first block I looked at here looks like subfloor gabbro.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Battery covers are awful dirty, but I will not dust them as long as you're happy.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't think dusting the battery covers gives us any cooler batteries.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I know; but it keeps the batteries from getting dust in them.
I've had pretty good luck with them.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You are dusted; and you're shiny bright.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Listen, if Ed thanks me, that's enough.
(Pause) A man couldn't ask for any more than that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think your LMP just ran away.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, I've got my thing...(Hearing Bob) Where'd he go?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I thought maybe you fell in that crater I'm looking at.
I'm going to give you a
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Our fender's beginning to fade and, uh-oh, the clip came off (the replacement fender but not the Rover) on the inside; that's what's wrong.
We'll have to fix that before we start.
The outside one's holding, but the inside one's not.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, all the blocks bigger than 20 centimeters that I've looked at up here are subfloor gabbro in appearance.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Did we get a mark there on the gravimeter, Gene?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Gene, I'm going to go up and look at this one rock.
Why don't you set up and sample any one of these other big ones.
Like the one near the Rover.
And I'll go up and try to get this big one down there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's the only one left to look at, but right now we're dealing with subfloor material, I think.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: What about some of these little fragments that seem to be sitting more on the surface?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, we're supposed to rake here.
We'll get those with the rake.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That one up there, by the way, is sitting on the surface.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's why I want to look at it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You don't have a hammer, but if you need me, I'll come up there because I think that may be worthwhile.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll roll it down to you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'd like to have you leaving here in three-zero minutes to make up some of the (extra) time we spent at Stations 6 and 7.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And we'd also remind you that we'd like a rake soil sample here, too.
That may be the only way we try and pick up some stuff other than subfloor, if that, indeed, has come down from the top of the Sculptured Hills.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: This rock is a big chunk of shattered, but still visible, bluish-gray anorthosite.
It's glass-coated, and it actually looks like it's vesicular.
I'm going to roll it downhill so we can work on it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: But the point is, as Gene said, it's the only rock - (the only) big one anyway - in the area that I see that's perched on the surface as if it might have rolled here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: But I don't see a (boulder) track.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, this one here is tough as a...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, we can get some small ones.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, that I'm going to do.
I tell you, this one is so...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I thought you might be able to break it up.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, there's no...there's no corners on it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Over here to the (garbled under Jack).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm not sure I am, but go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Look, I would roll on this slope, why don't you?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, I'll bet you they would like, if I didn't step on it, a sample out of the bottom of that thing.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: These others all look...You're right, Jack, they look like what we've been sampling (that is, the subfloor gabbro).
(Pause) And they're all pretty well mantled except the ones you got up there.
There's one more piece I see on the side of that crater that may not be.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bag 545 will be soil from under that anorthosite boulder.
(Pause) Bob, the only thing that bothers me about that boulder being subfloor - I mean Sculptured Hills - is that it's glass-coated.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It may have been thrown in here by an impact.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, let's sample it, and then roll it down.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I never would have moved it if I thought you were coming up.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, I wasn't coming up; but I looked at some of those others, and there's only one more...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I got it documented up in place.
Let's...That's not the...I think that's the side that was down.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Placing the gnomon) Well, let me get a piece of that side since it was underneath.
Then we'll roll it over and get a piece of the other side.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let's do it (take documentation photos) again.
Except I got dust all over it (meaning the rock).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Right of the rock) The albedo...The down-Sun picture's not going to mean much.
Let me get this (soil) sample in your bag.
(Pause; going to Gene) I think we ought to change your bag because the stuff's going to start flying out.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, after this one, there's only...There's one more in that crater (near the Rover).
It may be from that crater, but I don't know.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: This will be an easy one, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, that's a pretty one inside!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, it's stained by the glass coating.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's stained by that glass coating.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: While I'm at it, I'm going to chop another piece off right here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You've got three pieces laying around.
(Pause) Let's get those before we lose them.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh; you already got them in the bag?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We copy; 564 from the bottom of the boulder.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It (the fragment) got mixed with local soil.
I think I'd recognize the top, although it's got dust all over it now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think I'll get one more swap (rhymes with "wrap") off there.
I don't want to seal this.
Let me get another swap off there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We haven't seen anything like this!
Unless you've been holding out on me.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, this is a nice crystalline rock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Turning to look) Okay, I see that one.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll go get it with my tongs.
That one I worked too hard to get.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, I see how it (the Moon) makes boulder tracks!
It just skipped along (and) made those little pothole craters as it went.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: This is about a 50-50 mixture of what looks like maskelynite or at least blue-gray plagioclase, and a very, let's say, light yellow-tan mineral, probably orthopyroxene.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: When you guys get done with that rock, we'd like to get to the rake sample, please.
And that's probably just as well done by the Rover as anyplace else.
We don't seem to see anything worthwhile here doing besides that.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Referring to the fragment) You got it?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That went in the same bag, Bob, as the rest of the chips from the bottom.
All the chips from the bottom are in 464.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Here, let me roll it over.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Bob) By "coarsely crystalline", (I mean that) probably the average grain size will turn out to be about 3 or 4 millimeters, maybe - maybe half a centimeter.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, I got to go get a couple of pictures.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You gonna roll...Yeah, we've really gotten that one messed up.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If you'd hold your scoop where that one came off, it'd help.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Getting his scoop, which is planted to the left of the rock) Yeah; I was just going over there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That last one I took off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Positioning the scoop over the rock for Gene's "after") Right there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's move the gnomon, and we won't roll it over on the gnomon.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That other side is the one that was up.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Kicking the boulder over) Well, I'm not sure now.
It's got so much dust on it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's not going to roll down that hill unless we get it on edge.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, look at that glass on it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Which side was the glass on when you looked at it?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: 17, there's probably not much point in spending a lot of time out here trying to decide which is the top.
It's not big enough, anyway, really to worry about...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...the top and bottom samples that are radiologically significant.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, let's...(To Bob) If you don't want another sample, then we can go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, let me get a piece of this glass.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me try to get them.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay; a piece of the glass from it, Bob, is 546.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Along with a little of the local soil.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And now we're ready for you guys to rake...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...and I guess they suggest the crater rim if possible.
Probably over there near the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Gene) Now you got a sample of that big block down there, huh?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Too bad I don't have my skis!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, did you get a pan up here?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Little hard to get a good hip rotation.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's see, I must be looking back at...Well, there's SWP.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm looking back at the complex - Cochise and Shakespeare - and I can see the LM.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: One interesting thing up here, you can see the erosional pattern of the talus (or) the mantle that - I call it a mantle, but (I mean) the talus - that's on the Sculptured Hills.
There's little...little boulder tracks of all sizes from (means "made by") all these little clods.
And they all, of course, point downhill or nearly downhill.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: In the interest of time, I'll document this (rake sample) without the gnomon.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I presume Gene's got the gnomon up there?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I should have brought it, but...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Grabbing the gnomon and starting downhill) Don't forget the Gene, gnomon!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, when you do this, and you're going downslope, that first step is a long one!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Doing long, two-footed hops) This is the best way for me to travel.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And on level ground, I can skip.
I don't like that loping thing.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, the loping's the only way to go.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well...See, when I'm on level ground, I can skip.
But this two-legged thing is great!
Man, I can cover ground like a kangaroo!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You documented already; I was just going to put this (gnomon) in the field-of-view anyway.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: On the "after" we can have it there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, what do you think about that?
There's not much (that is, no rocks) in here worth...Man, there's just nothing...This has been totally mantled with talus.
Well, it is; because that downhill pattern goes right down the slope of this crater, and...Actually, it goes upslope of the crater!
This may be on a ray somewhere.
Because it goes right downhill...This little, skinny boulder-trail pattern goes right up the slope.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Still raking) I think those are later than the crater by a long ways.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Did you sample anything over here?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to pick up the piece out of that little...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll just take it to it.
(Pause) Let me know when you're ready for a bag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Having taken about 15 to 20 short swaths to this point) Well, I'm about ready.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Bob) I raked about a 2-meter square area - maybe...yeah, about 2 meters - and down to 4 or 5 centimeters for these.
(To Gene) They all going to go in?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: They're all in; (starts to give Bob the bag number)...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Sounds like a good rake sample for a change.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And this is a "kilogram-of-soil" location, fellas.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Jack, your bag (SCB) is full; we're going to have...No, it isn't, but we ought to change it when we get back there (at the Rover), anyway.
And that one ought to go under your seat.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll be ready to take it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Getting a sample bag ready) The kilogram is in 566.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) And, remaining here, we'd have primarily a trench, if you fellas think it's feasible.
We'd like to be moving in one-one minutes.
(Pause) And we could use a pan from this lower location also, probably.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Jack) Why don't you go back and dig a trench at the Rover?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And we also remind you of getting...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Jack, once you get a trench at the Rover...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...a pan at the lower section there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) I'll get the sample here that I got documented now and...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Did you...Is that all going to go in there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That rock may have been too much.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) We're going to have to put it in mine, though.
Since we're going to unload your bag, this may be the last one.
That's the last one for your bag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Did you get anything out of that little crater?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Going into a meter-deep crater just north of the rake-sample site) No.
But I'm going to right now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Why don't you get your "after" picture (of the rake site) over there and go down and get that trench.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I can do it myself here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, almost pure white and very friable.
Right out of a small little pit crater on the side of this crater I just walked in, Houston.
And it's pure white, very friable.
I got about, well, one big piece and several small in 567.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (On his way to the Rover) Bob, the walls of the big craters around here - that is, the ones that are, say, 15 meters in diameter - tend to be a little bit lighter albedo than ones down in the mantled area.
(Pause) I'm afraid those pictures on that rake may be through a dust-colored lens.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, they were also in my documented sample here, too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Hopping toward the Rover, almost tripping) Okay.
Where do you want this trench?
On the side of this crater?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I was just thinking about that.
I think we ought to get out in the inter crater area to see if there's any stratigraphy to whatever the talus is.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to leave the gnomon right here...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, while you're digging that trench, we've got the pan to get, but I want to fix this fender.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I guess the pan's mine, isn't it, this one?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And I want to fix the fender before...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We agree with that, and you might get us...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...you might get us the gravimeter reading there, Gene, while you're at it.
And if you have time, you might drop the gravimeter on the ground, and we'll get a reading with it on the ground as well.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You want it dropped on the ground, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I can't find a general level spot, but I'll level it.
If it takes pictures...(correcting himself), or, if it does it's thing on the Rover (which isn't very level), it'll do its thing here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, this is just to get a check...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It's fender-fixing time; it's camera-taking-off time.
(Pause) And I think I'll zap myself with a little cool water.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And how's the trench going, Jack?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: When you call for cold water, does it come in nicely.
(Pause) I'm really happy with this fender, really happy with it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, I have gotten a wall now, in one place, that's standing about 25 centimeters high.
And it shows no apparent change in the texture of the soil to that depth; except possibly at the lower 5 centimeters, there's some zones that might be slightly more granular.
Particle size may be up a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Probably just three samples will be sufficient, then.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Be there in a minute, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Boy, we're sure giving this suspension system a workout.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Everything is just full of dust.
There's got to be a point where the dust just overtakes you, and everything mechanical quits moving.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm not sure whether Detroit would like the fender, but it will sure buy the fix.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I'm happy; I like it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And we'd like to have you guys moving in about 3 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You need any help (garbled) bag those samples, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I can't adjust my scoop to my self-bagging method.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Going to Jack) Let me get back on some lighter cooling here, too, to save some water.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get your bags...I left my camera off when I...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I didn't take a picture of the trench after I dug it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Backing up to take pictures) That's the bottom.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Looks very much like the surface we're standing on except it clods up quite a bit more.
Can you tell anything from the trench itself?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I told them...I talked to them a little bit about it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It looked a little coarser-grained, but that's all.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It sure holds a nice wall, though.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's the kind of wall I expect those core tubes held.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Skim of the upper...We'll see how well I do.
(It's a) skim sample of the upper half centimeter.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to put it in your bag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Presenting his SCB) Is it going to fit in there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, there's no choice, right now.
Let me...Yeah, these little ones will fit in there.
I want to put this one in there, too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Sealing the bag and putting it in Jack's SCB) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, the upper...Below that skim: the next 5 centimeters.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, just put it (the scoop) over it (the bag).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I can't turn it (the scoop).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Scraping a deeper section of the trench wall) And the next 10 centimeters down...(Pause) (To Gene) Can you get this one too?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Now, I got to get your bag (SCB).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That was the next 10 centimeters; and, then, the first sample, of course, was the 10 centimeters below that.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We're ready for you guys to move out.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You didn't get a pan here.
While I clean up the Rover, you can get your "after" of the trench in the pan.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: What's the key that keeps...I keep getting keyed.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It sounds like Bob's stepping on his foot mike.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Getting the gnomon and heading for the gate) You get your pan, and I'll get the
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You took a pan up the hill there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah; I took it way up there, somewhere.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll take it right here, then.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If you can't get it, I'll get it with the tongs.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Go ahead and go to work, and I'll get the pan first.
I lost two of them, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, those are the last two I put in there.
Your bag is so full they won't stay.
Let me give them a reading here.
Hey, Bob, can I move it on the Rover and then give you a reading?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: As long as you're careful not to hit the button while you're doing it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Responding to Bob) I won't hit the button.
Just easier to do it that way.
I don't know why I asked you; I know I can.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I've got to dust that thing (the
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll get those things with my tongs.
Every time you jump around, you come close to losing something.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Arriving with the tongs and picking up the first sample bag) I'll just take them back there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) You want me to take that one?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Now getting the second sample bag) No, I got it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You got another one dropped there, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Jack, we've got to make a place in here for that full bag.
Let me put the small can over there, and core tube over there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Holding the sample, still in the scoop, up to the TV) Have a sample.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me take your bag off first.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, you might as well fill it as full as you can.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Marveling at the weight of Jack's SCB as he starts to take it off) Holy Smoley!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Your bag isn't in much better shape.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'd like to have you check the Commander's bag.
You might put them both under the seat there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, we're running out of bags, aren't we?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We've got one bag left...we should have there.
It was on the gate, right?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We had put it (the SCB which had been on the gate) under the seat.
Okay, bag number 4 (which Jack has been wearing) is absolutely full.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I suggest that you take the other bag that's on the gate there, and put that on either you or Jack.
And also, the Commander's bag is pretty full also, we suspect.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Why don't you put it on me?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You might check Gene's bag anyway.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'm trying to get the bottom hook.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: He's got about six samples to go.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And I just want to be sure that it's locked down.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, turn to the left so I can get this other hook.
It's not coming out; I guarantee you that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And SCB-5 is one for the LMP if you want to take it off the gate.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Turning so that Jack can check his SCB) SCB-5 is on the LMP.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There is nothing on the gate.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I think that (Gene's SCB top)'ll stay down, but it's not very good...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I've got one more loose sample I'm going to throw in the big bag back there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Referring to the loose sample) A local one, you mean?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, let me leave it under your seat.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, let's...Can I put a bag around it?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, it's got a bag around it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Jack, while Gene's doing that, why don't you read the SEP temperature, or somebody read the SEP temperature anyway, and close the blankets.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Let's see, you got your (gravimeter) readings...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Those blankets just aren't staying closed.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I guess we're ready to head on out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Gene, when you go to change the LCRU, we'd like you to turn it to Off.
O-F-F, on the Power switch, the Internal Power, External switch.
And we'll be reading you through the LM.
It will give you a chance to cool down the LCRU on the way home to Station 9.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And, Houston, what's the temperature limit on the DSEA (the SEP Data Storage and Electronics Assembly)?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Do you read us, Bob, through the LM?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We read you through the LM.
Do you read us through the LM?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Not as well, but we're reading you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And the temperature limit, Jack, is 160.
We'll just leave it as is until we get back to the LM.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I was going to say, we could take it out and put it under the seat or something, but that sounds all right.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm at 3.88 (psi suit pressure), and I got 48 percent (oxygen), no (warning) flags, and I'm
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And the LMP is at 47 percent, no flags, 3.86.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well...Bob, I guess...Remind us to change the LRV Sampler (the "Dixie cup" sampler) at the next station.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, let's do it next time around.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: When you get on, Jack, you can give me a frame count as you start moving.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) (To Gene, who has fallen) Hang on.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Watch it, there's a crater right behind you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, now, just push up on my head.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm not going to do it too hard.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's all right; just push up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Chuckling) Boy, are you...(Chuckling) You('ve) got your pockets completely filled with dirt.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Do we throw those pockets away this time around?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Laughs) Well, that one was coming for a long time!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: My hands are already tired from dusting you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I keep trying to blow the dust off my camera, which is very frustrating.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Preparing to mount the Rover again) Okay.
Do we try that trick again?
(Long Pause) You know that happened on an upslope getting on the Rover.
Let me know when you are.
How come we haven't deployed any charges?
I guess the last one...I remember when that one is.
transcript.speaker: Cernan
